status: DRAFT — store v2 course 6-of-8 by rank. All scenes source-mapped and VTT-verified. KEY CORRECTIONS: chapters 1-4 are a single-source cut (WlqQChOychs 2024-05-23 — the manifest's 'really-really-good / intraday-tactics family' attribution was wrong); the cut track's 'DIA 2023-07-12 single outcome' DOES NOT VERIFY (no symbol named in the VWAP walk — whiteboard hypothetical); Ken's on-camera decision tree has exactly TWO spoken branches.
title: The Intraday Decision Tree (v2)
topic_class: mechanical (most decidable course in the catalog)
notes: Cut track: docs/store-v2-cut-tracks.md §5. Outcome studies to run BEFORE VO: pyramid-vs-flat R (QQQ/IWM day-trend days), rangestat no-back-inside trail, big-gap RL10 fade (gapz >= +1sigma), VWAP-magnet fadestat reversion (SPY+DIA 1-min) — plus the 5DD aggregate ALREADY COMPUTED. The scene-3 single outcome stays CONDITIONAL: symbol not named in audio (fingerprints for frame OCR: member chart ~$80->86 w/ MACD seasons spring->summer->fall; Ken's own example is from the session date 2024-05-23, 3-min SSC through VWAP/BB mean). Decision-tree explainer must attribute only the two spoken branches (small gap -> O3 'train station'; big gap -> RL10 bracket immediately) to Ken; the slow-trend and choppy branches are editorial extension and get labeled as such.

segments.label: OVERDUB: don't exit on the wiggle (+ conditional single outcome)

segments.label: Chapter 2 card
Respect the P -> ★ the zero-defect exit (one contiguous passage)
segments.status: MAPPED-VERIFIED — v1 split this single 65s beat across three scenes; ★ verbatim at 970-985; sting ON the line
segments.quote_anchor: that P never got hit by price... make sure that you respect the P as the zero-defect exit... don't ever let price go below the PSAR, particularly after you have an abnormally good win

segments.label: Layering judgment on top (dragon-skin acceleration)
segments.status: MAPPED (strongest of the three judgment passages; 976-990 'exit higher than the P because you have an intuition, that's fine' available as intro splice)
segments.quote_anchor: trusting the PSAR all the way up... then I accelerate my stop to the skin of the Dragon... 80% of the time

segments.label: EXPLAINER: the decision tree (two branches are Ken's; extensions labeled)
segments.vo_script: TO WRITE. ATTRIBUTION RULE from mapping: Ken's spoken tree has exactly two branches — small gap: use the O3, wait at the train station; big gap: RL10 bracket, trade it immediately, not even waiting three minutes. The slow-trend-continuation and choppy-no-trade branches are editorial extensions and the VO says so plainly ('the course adds two more branches from his broader teaching').
segments.animation_brief: Branching-path animation (hero art branching_paths.png is the seed); Ken-verbatim branches drawn solid, editorial branches drawn distinct + labeled.
segments.quote_anchor: now what this sets up then is a decision tree — when to use the O3, when to use the r10 bracket

segments.label: OVERDUB: big-gap default + gap stat (no predictive direction)

segments.quote_anchor: if it's a large gap then chances are all of the traders are in an exaggerated state of mind... the direction of the gap has no predictive value
